extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won after fighting for STUYVEN Jasper's wheel through final 2km, launched on 12% cobbled ramp with 200m remaining.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN fought physically with DEGENKOLB for STUYVEN's wheel final 2km, prioritizing position over saving energy before steep finish.
Trek-Segafredo controlled final 2km for STUYVEN leadout, exposing SAGAN needed their train more than own teammates.
12% cobbled ramp final 200m after left bend rewards riders who secure position early, not pure sprinters launching late.
SAGAN changed bike twice under 20km out, BODNAR paced back—team willing to burn domestique despite sprint finish looming.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: mechanicals didn't compromise final positioning, power on steep cobbled ramp superior to pure sprinters
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: neutral: finished second after overhauling DEGENKOLB but lacked positioning through final 2km to challenge SAGAN
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: Trek leadout valuable enough that rivals fought physically for his wheel rather than organizing own trains
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: negative: had STUYVEN wheel but lost physical battle to SAGAN, then faded to third behind VIVIANI
rider_signals.BODNAR Maciej: neutral: burned pacing SAGAN back from second mechanical with 20km left, reducing sprint support options
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: neutral: sits second overall 10 seconds down, not contesting sprint suggests GC focus over stage hunting
